---
title: "Lesson8_R_Array"
author: "Paul Lott"
date: "2024-05-13"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Array

R arrays extend the idea of matrices 2d datasets, to store more than two dimensions. You can use arrays to create higher dimensional datasets.

`array(vector, dim=)`

Because they are kind of an extension of matrices, I'm going to skip over them for now.

If you need to access array information you can use the indices `[row, col, matrix]` operators.

```{r}
vector1 <- 1:50

# Take these vectors as input to the array.
result <- array(vector1,dim = c(2,5,5))
print(result)
print(result[1,3,3])
```
